pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.
verification_questions: Does the latest available information materially change the decision, given: Round 13 and any official FIDE standings/result update can move the probability materially.?

How decision-relevant is the remaining contract/source-of-truth ambiguity: official winner not yet declared; current confidence relies partly on contextual live-standings reporting rather than a clean official standings capture?
Are the current blockers still material to final judgment: No clean official FIDE standings/clinch page was captured directly in the synthesis pass?
Is the main verification gap still decision-material: I did not obtain a clean official FIDE live standings or clinch page directly in the synthesis pass.?

structured_handoff_focus.contract_ambiguity_reason: official winner not yet declared; current confidence relies partly on contextual live-standings reporting rather than a clean official standings capture
structured_handoff_focus.verification_gap_summary: I did not obtain a clean official FIDE live standings or clinch page directly in the synthesis pass.
structured_handoff_focus.best_countercase_summary: If the live-state interpretation is overstated or Sindarov loses round 13, the race can reopen quickly and 95% was too high.
structured_handoff_focus.main_reason_for_disagreement: Different weighting of missing direct official standings verification versus strong contextual late-stage lead evidence.
structured_handoff_focus.resolution_mechanics_summary: Resolve to the official FIDE-declared winner, with consensus credible reporting only as fallback.
structured_handoff_focus.freshness_driver: Round 13 and any official FIDE standings/result update can move the probability materially.
decision_blockers: No clean official FIDE standings/clinch page was captured directly in the synthesis pass
The tournament was not yet officially finished at analysis time

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market is directionally right because Sindarov appears to have an almost decisive late-stage lead, but 95.05% is slightly too high given residual two-round, tiebreak, and official-live-verification risk.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 95.05% market price as a serious prior.
Ask what would justify that price: an overwhelming late-stage live lead rather than generic pre-event dominance.
Contextual standings indicate exactly that state, with Sindarov on 9/12 and nearest chaser on 7/12 after 12 rounds.
Because FIDE is the resolution authority and two rounds remain, trim modestly for residual conversion and verification risk.
strongest_supports: Contextual live standings show Sindarov leading by two points with two rounds left.
The tournament is only 14 rounds, so there are few remaining opportunities for the field to catch up.
strongest_disconfirmers: Two rounds plus possible tie-breaks still leave a nonzero failure path.
Official FIDE live standings were not cleanly extracted in this run, so some confidence relies on contextual sources.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official live standings were not directly captured from a clean FIDE page in this run.
Residual tie scenarios depend on remaining round outcomes not yet played.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: A clean official FIDE standings/result update showing a smaller lead, a round-13 stumble, or any procedural dispute would push the estimate down materially.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: Very high; this is a late-stage in-progress tournament market where one round can change the entire tail-risk profile.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: Resolution authority is high-quality and clear via FIDE, while the live-state evidence is strong but more contextual than ideal, so overall source quality is medium-high rather than fully high.

frontmatter.topic: market confidence versus field strength in 2026 FIDE Candidates
frontmatter.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?

selected_assumption_snippets.snippet:
# Assumption
The market's 95.05% price is implicitly assuming either that Javokhir Sindarov is an overwhelming pre-tournament favorite versus seven elite qualifiers or that substantial contract/listing context outside the visible rules makes his victory close to settled.
## Why this assumption matters
Without that assumption, a pre-event price above 95% in an eight-player double round-robin is extremely hard to justify. The whole risk-manager view turns on whether this confidence comes from real hidden information or from market mispricing / market-structure distortion.
## What this assumption supports
- A view that the displayed market price is likely too confident.
- A probability estimate materially below the market-implied baseline.
- Elevated concern about hidden assumptions, field-strength underweighting, or book-structure issues.
## Evidence or logic behind the assumption
- Official FIDE page confirms a full eight-player field rather than a one-opponent match.
- The format is long and variance-exposed: 14 rounds plus playoff if tied.
- The official field includes multiple world-class players, so the base-rate path to 95% for any one player appears implausible absent extraordinary form or unseen disqualifying constraints.
- Polymarket rules do not show any special shortcut that would make Sindarov nearly locked absent actual tournament play.
## What would falsify it
- Evidence that this market is being quoted after the tournament has largely been played and Sindarov already leads by an almost insurmountable margin.
- Official evidence that several listed rivals are no …
